scope.summary: Commits the C8 fixture: a mock connection that models dictionary visibility as a property of the principal, a green test proving a sighted principal on a clean table still gets a read-only proof (and that both probes run), and a committed-red test asserting a catalog-blind principal must yield Purity::Unknown instead of falling through to ProvenReadOnly.
out_of_scope: Requiring positive catalog visibility — A1a owns the fix; this bead owns only the fixture and deliberately does not model A1a's chosen control mechanism.

Per-view partial blindness (readable ALL_POLICIES but unreadable ALL_TAB_COLS). Realistic under per-view grants, but modelling it requires guessing A1a's probe shape, which would make the fixture unflippable.
Any change to the two probe SQLs, to Purity, or to the caller that consumes the verdict.

entry_points.name: resolved_relations_read_purity — the SELECT-side purity proof behind guarded reads
entry_points.trace: c8_a_sighted_principal_on_a_clean_table_still_proves_read_only drives the real resolver against a mock connection, asserts ProvenReadOnly, and asserts that both SELECT_POLICY_SQL and VIRTUAL_COLUMN_SQL were actually issued — pinning that the red case covers both gates rather than the first short-circuiting

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --lib (453 passed, 2 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.
The blind-principal assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run; it is not cited as proof of anything here.

The fixture proves the resolver returns ProvenReadOnly for a blind principal. It does NOT execute a real VPD-filtered read end to end, so the downstream consequence (silently filtered rows returned with exit-success) is reasoned from the call site rather than demonstrated live. A live proof needs the D1 container harness with a VPD policy and a least-privilege principal.
Only total dictionary blindness is modelled. Per-view partial blindness is realistic under individual grants and is left to A1a, whose probe design determines how to express it.
follow_ups: A1a must delete the #[ignore] on c8_a_catalog_blind_principal_must_not_yield_a_read_only_proof, leaving the assertion unchanged, and must not satisfy it by returning Unknown unconditionally — c8_a_sighted_principal_on_a_clean_table_still_proves_read_only is the guard against that.
A1a must deliberately update relation_purity_requires_plain_policy_free_non_virtual_tables, which pins queries.len() == 2 and the exact probe order.
